<!-- version: 1.1.0 -->
# Current PR Contract

This contract constrains implementation scope for the active PR. Update
it when scope is explicitly amended. If a requested action falls outside
approved scope, stop and escalate before proceeding.

Use this contract to distinguish active PR constraints, completed PR
constraints, durable invariants, and intentional amendments. Completed
PR constraints are historical evidence unless they are explicitly
promoted to durable invariants.

## Goal
Install cARL governance runtime into UndREST-SpecQL and establish durable
agent governance artefacts so that future coding agents operate with
persistent architectural memory, scoped PR contracts, and consistent
engineering discipline.

## Contract status
active

## Non-goals
- Do not refactor product code (SpeQL.py, analyze.py, refresh_database.py, run-queries.sh, or export scripts).
- Do not change query behaviour or CodeQL query logic.
- Do not rebuild the CodeQL database.
- Do not regenerate inventory, shard, or SARIF artefacts.
- Do not vendor external dependencies (azure-rest-api-specs).
- Do not upgrade CodeQL CLI or pin a new version.
- Do not add Python packages to requirements.txt.

## Carry-forward rules
The following constraints from this PR are promoted to durable invariants and must persist into all future PRs:
- Always pin CodeQL CLI to 2.20.1 or 2.20.2 (JSON-only database compatibility).
- Do not commit database/, results/, azure-rest-api-specs/, or generated inventory JSON files.
- requirements.txt lists only runtime-required Python packages; pytest is documented separately.
- `carl doctor` must remain healthy after any changes to `.github/carl/` artefacts.

## Approved scope
- Add the cARL runtime artefacts generated by `carl init` under `.github/carl/`, `.github/instructions/`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.cursor/`, and `.agents/`.
- Populate `.github/carl/memory.md` with SpecQL-specific architecture truth (replacing the cARL-repo boilerplate installed by `carl init`).
- Create `.github/carl/current-pr-contract.md` (this file).
- Update `.gitignore` if harness shim files need exclusion entries (none required; all are committed).
- Add a cARL usage note to README.md and/or CONTRIBUTING.md if needed to document how future contributors interact with cARL.

## Intentional amendments
None. This is a greenfield cARL installation — no prior governance artefacts exist to amend.

## Forbidden scope
- Modifying SpeQL.py, analyze.py, refresh_database.py, run-queries.sh, or any script under scripts/.
- Modifying CodeQL queries under queries/.
- Modifying GitHub Actions workflows under .github/workflows/.
- Modifying config/sources/azure.json or other source configs.
- Adding, removing, or changing Python packages in requirements.txt.
- Touching database/, results/, inventory/, or azure-rest-api-specs/ directories.
- Generating or deleting any SARIF, JSON index, or shard output.

## Architectural constraints
- SpeQL architecture and product behaviour must remain identical before and after this PR.
- cARL artefacts are governance files only — they do not alter script execution or query logic.
- `.github/carl/memory.md` must reflect SpecQL's actual architecture, not cARL-repo boilerplate.
- `carl doctor` must report healthy after all changes are committed.

## Security constraints
- No secrets, tokens, or credentials may be committed.
- No new external network dependencies are introduced.
- cARL governance files are static markdown/JSON/YAML — they do not execute code.

## Files expected to change
- `.github/carl/memory.md` — replaced with SpecQL-specific architecture truth
- `.github/carl/current-pr-contract.md` — this file (created)
- `.github/carl/` (entire directory) — new; generated by `carl init`
- `.github/instructions/` (entire directory) — new; generated by `carl init`
- `.github/copilot-instructions.md` — new; cARL harness loader
- `CLAUDE.md` — new; cARL harness shim
- `AGENTS.md` — new; cARL harness shim
- `.cursor/` — new; cARL harness shim
- `.agents/` — new; cARL harness shim
- `README.md` — minor addition (cARL governance section)
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` — minor addition (cARL usage note)

## Tests / validation
- `carl version` — CLI 0.4.2, Runtime 1.0.0, Status: Healthy ✓
- `carl doctor` — INFO: runtime is healthy — all managed artefacts are present and canonical ✓
- `python3 -m pytest tests/test_api_inventory_export.py tests/test_api_inventory_normalization.py -v` — 120/120 passed ✓
- Tests requiring CodeQL CLI, azure-rest-api-specs clone, or a built database were not run (external dependencies unavailable in this environment). This is expected and documented.

## Stop conditions
- Any change that alters product script or CodeQL query behaviour.
- Any generated database, inventory, or results file appearing in git status.
- `carl doctor` reporting unhealthy after changes.
- Any secret detected in changed files.

## Escalation triggers
- If a required cARL artefact conflicts with existing SpecQL conventions, stop and report before proceeding.
- If `carl init` or `carl doctor` output differs from documented expected output, report the difference.

## Context reset notes
This contract covers only the initial cARL installation. Once merged, close this contract and create a new one per subsequent PR. Durable invariants listed above are carried forward into memory.md and invariants.yml — they are not lost when this contract is closed.
